item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It says that 166 out of 173 test subjects got English words, and the other 9 got the eye. But 173 - 166 is 7. Intentional?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The first image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-S 3.0) as the image comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, it has been removed. The second image comes from here and is Public Domain, thus cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, the dreaded ALSOTAPER, the fiercest creature known to man. Legend foretells that any who meet its terrifying gaze will crumble to ash on the spot, and that their souls will be forfeit in the next life.

Seriously, I've been trying for 10 minutes and I can't figure out what that one means. Upvoting for 'teh mysterees', but still…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remind me to remember Google exists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/In_situ

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is what it means, though it is almost always italicized.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Testing may be performed in situ

Did you mean "in site" or "on site"?

item.title: Pretty neat
item.content:encoded: I'm new here so i'm not quite that experienced with giving meaningful critique, but personally i found this to be well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the result of Experiment 1172-C a Captain Beefheart reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only just got the double word score pun in the title.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this paragraph:

Removal of the tiles from the bag must be performed in accordance with strict protocol:

• Removed tiles must not be taken more than 30cm from the bag. Absolute safe limit has been recorded at 54cm.
• Once a tile is taken from the bag at least six others must also be removed. They must in turn be placed to the immediate right of the previous tile.
• No more than ten tiles are to be removed per use, after which all tiles must be returned to the bag.

Should be in containment procedures, not description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Fortean Times is a magazine dedicated to "anomalous phenomena". Reader-submitted editorials are usually along the lines of the one in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: really loved this! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really don't see why this was featured. It seems really weird in terms of effects and just seems boring in general.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Confused, very weak on the punctuation and all around not that interesting to me.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it was because of the first image being a Scrabble bag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
173-166 is not nine.

Other than that, this is great. I'd love more test logs, but +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+2, apparently.

Needs more test logs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs more test logs
This SCP deserved it, I love this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like what Saya-tan said, I don't get the point of the Alala reference (unless there's plans to connect it with more skips with similar references). Also, did the way it was discovered bug anyone else? I doubt a publication would greenlight drug use references, and I doubt anyone would actually think someone's had a similar experience to something so specifically odd, much less what seems to be a newspaper.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but I have two gripes:

a) I don't understand what Alala has to do with anything
b) I found the effect of the eye tiles sufficiently clear without the Addendum, which feels redundant

+1, regardless

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i generally like this but it feels…confused. it seems to be the remains of several scp ideas shoved together into something generally well written but ultimately confused. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to upvote because why not (which is my general M.O.), but I have two complaints, one structural, one conceptual:

The description of the anomalous properties of a scip should be, well, in the description. The only thing in the description apart from "ghost eye tiles" is the reference to "being touched", and it doesn't even elaborate - I guessed it was being touched by another set of fingers or something, I didn't know what to think, and I actually felt consciously irritated by the fact that I didn't know what to think. Recovery logs and test logs can contain information very vital to a scip, that's actually pretty common, but a general description of all anomalous things should be at least summarized in the description.

As for the concept…It's weird, it's interesting, but it's kind of scattered. No one anomalous property has any real direct connection to any other anomalous property. You have ghost eye tiles, the feeling of being touched by a fish, screams of a Greek goddess of war cries, bullet holes when tiles get too far away, and prophetic Scrabble. These things are loosely connected at best. Bottom line, what the actual hell is going on here? For vagueness in SCP articles, there's vague enough to make the reader imagine things creepier than anything the author could actually say outright, and there's vague enough to make the reader ask WTF without having any straws for the imagination to grab at, and this article is leaning towards the latter. That's not good. Something to tie all this random stuff together would make this excellent; as it is, it's just…meh. Still gets my upvote, but probably not everyone's.

Also, "B E H I N D Y O U" makes me think of the Slender Man. There is nowhere else my mind can go when given that line. It's good that it's gone, but I looked for the "end bit" mentioned in the discussion hoping to find a clue as to what the heck is going on with this Scrabble bag, and instead got a reminder of the Slender Man, which has nothing to do with this thing whatsoever. Just saying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if a test subject takes those tiles?

I A M D E A D [eye tile]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "In situ" is a Latin phrase meaning "in position" and is perfectly acceptable in the context provided by the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, the containment procedures, you misspelled site as "situ"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent suggestions, I've edited to account for both.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Couple of grammar nitpicks: The word criteria is plural, so it should be “these criteria were,” not “this criteria was,” after the bulleted list. Also, you can't really “breach” criteria; the last sentence of that paragraph, which starts with “Any breaches of this criteria,” should probably say “breaches of this protocol” or simply “breaches” or “violations” instead.

item.title: doc?
item.content:encoded: maybe you should add more tests, but put them in a seperate document. maybe all 177. other people can help

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the interesting complications to the concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like all of it, except for the part about Alala. The greek mythology part seems kind of out of place with the rest of the article, and doesn't seem to serve any purpose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I may rewrite the boreholes, my original though was that the boreholes are caused by the bag thrashing about for the tiles, and they get pulled back over time by the bag itself.

And the surprising thing about the eyetile was that I got the idea when I found a picture of a Scrabble tile with an eye on it, whilst googling for the main picture. I decided on a drawing because I wanted the tiles to be even weirder than the normal ones.

As for the D-class, of course they shot him! It wasn't relevant to the report though, so it wasn't in the interview fragment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The boreholes thing really doesn't seem to fit, and I'm not sure how you're supposed to put the tiles back if you'll die trying to get to them.

My guess is the thing about the eye tiles not showing up in photographs is only there as an excuse for not having an actual picture.

Also, some of the dialog is clunky or just doesn't fit with the Foundation. They couldn't convince the guy to stick his hand in the bag? No threats of revoking his release, or just to shoot him for non-compliance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ugh. I can't believe I didn't notice that sooner. Well played. You have my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like trying to decipher messages from Inglip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check how the bag was dyed. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite being a fan of both, to my disappointment, I cannot find them.
EDIT: The Scrabble scene from Hitchhiker's came back to me, but I still can't see the Minecraft one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're right. An earlier draft read 'inciting racial violence', the word must have stuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it help that in an early draft they were incredibly fine tentacles, but that got cut? So it could have been worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It means the former, but it isn't immediately clear, you're right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like everything about this but the random boreholes.

+1 anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These tiles feature no score numbers.

Confused by this. Do you mean none of the tiles have score numbers, or just the eye ones? If the former, you need to tweak the picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-2532 was incarcerated in 1993 for a series of racially incited murders.

That should be "racially motivated".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, I'm letting in some blinders today. Fixed, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first test breaks your own rules. H A T E T H E M A L L is ELEVEN tiles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Speaking as a big scrabble fan, I really like this. Good show. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, that's not a paragraph! I was promised a paragraph! /feels snubbed

Ah well, it just makes me want more. (And the more I want, the more interested I am, huehuehue) So I'm content.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an extra two lines at the bottom of the second interview. I'm loathe to put much more detail in there, what you don't see is more frightening and all that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still don't see it. Am I blind?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have very little interest in writing Euclid or Keter articles, I'm a big fan of Hellraiser and the fact that the Lament Configuration box would be Safe makes my day.
If I told you what A L S O T A P E R really meant you'd be very disappointed, so yes, go with that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for making this Safe. And I can't help but read "A L S O T A P E R" and just think that he wanted to get ahead in the world, and can't get over the fact that he was stuck in a dead-end warehouse/shipping area that all he did was tape up and cut open boxes all day long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then hang on to your hat, because there's a paragraph that should have been in there that somehow got lost in the cut 'n' paste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This rustles my jimmies in a pretty good way. I really would like more description on the wet fish thing in the bag, though. It seems rather put there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah, I did think about wording that differently, but then I decided he was just the natural enemy of Robin Sparkles and let it stay.

Edit: Thanks - I checked those because of the amount of cut/paste I was doing, and missed it completely. Fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I-i am amusing myself by imagining the nazi guy just really disliked shopping malls

Edit: Oh snap actually the last line in the D-2532 interview has the next D-classes' number in place of his own

Good article though! Interesting and mysterious and mmhmm

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your (I presume impending) upvote is appreciated, I was so sure that there must be something that spat out predicted words somewhere in the site, but the only similar stuff I could find was mostly computerised or mechanical. It was also an opportunity to get both a Minecraft and a Hitchhikers reference into the same SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Admittedly, I was beginning to get creeped out before I hit that point. It only drove the paranoia home.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this a lot! +1 for scprabble.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "prophetic" is not a very scientific or even clinical term. Try "predictive", as per the tags.
